evaluation.summary: Boundary truncation markedly affects the variance ratio R(k) of primorial gaps, showing systematic variation that stabilizes at higher truncation levels for k≥3.
next_directions: Extend the analysis to higher primorial indices (k>6) with more statistical replicates.
Quantify measurement uncertainties and perform error propagation to assess significance of observed variations.
Compare the empirical R(k) trends against higher-order logarithmic and power‑law theoretical corrections.
